meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing Strategies to Scale Your First App
content.seo_description: Break past basic app limitations. Learn how to implement professional SaaS pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code in this beginner-friendly guide.
content.answer_block: Moving beyond basic apps requires a robust monetization layer. By integrating Stripe via Antigravity and orchestrating your backend logic with Claude Code, you transform a 'project' into a 'product.' Implement tiered subscription logic early to validate your value proposition and prove your engineering maturity to future employers.
content.intro:
You’ve built the to-do list, the weather app, and the basic CRUD interface. Yet, you feel stuck in a loop of toy projects that never see a real user or a single cent of revenue. The gap between a student project and a production-grade SaaS isn't just code complexity—it's the architecture of value exchange.

Transitioning from 'coder' to 'builder' requires mastering the business logic that keeps software alive. By architecting a professional pricing strategy, you stop building features for yourself and start solving problems for users willing to pay. This tutorial bridges that gap, leveraging modern AI tooling to handle the heavy lifting of payment infrastructure.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Establish a three-tier pricing model (Free, Pro, Enterprise). This forces you to think about feature gating and user permissions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a SaaS platform including 'users', 'subscriptions', and 'feature_flags' tables to support tiered access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --schema 'users(id, email), subscriptions(user_id, tier, status), feature_flags(tier, feature_name)'

steps.title: Bootstrap Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the Stripe integration, ensuring secure token handling and webhook listeners.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Node.js backend route for Stripe Checkout sessions that maps specific price IDs to user subscription tiers.

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Protect your premium features with middleware that checks the user's subscription status before executing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware function that validates a user's subscription tier against requested premium API endpoints.

steps.title: Automate Webhook Handling
steps.description: Ensure your database stays in sync with Stripe events like 'invoice.payment_succeeded' or 'customer.subscription.deleted'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust webhook handler for Stripe events that updates the local database subscription status automatically.

steps.title: Validate with Analytics
steps.description: Add basic telemetry to see which features users interact with most, helping you iterate on your pricing tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a lightweight event tracker to log user feature usage to a local analytics table for later pricing optimization.

pro_tips: Always offer a 'Free Tier' that provides genuine utility; it builds the user base required for eventual conversion.
Never hardcode your pricing IDs; store them in environment variables or a configuration service to allow for quick adjustments.
Focus on 'per-seat' or 'per-usage' pricing early to understand how your architecture scales with user growth.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your webhook handler to prevent immediate service disruption on failed payments.
Treat your pricing page as a conversion funnel; use A/B testing early, even with just 10 users, to learn what copy converts.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, deploy your application to a staging environment and run a 'smoke test' by mocking Stripe webhooks to ensure your database updates correctly.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to move beyond basic apps and into the realm of professional SaaS development. By implementing a real revenue model, you've leveled up your portfolio and your mindset. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
